name: Cohere Transcribe Arabic
tagline: State-of-the-art Arabic speech recognition — and actually Apache
description: A 2B-parameter speech-to-text model built for Arabic's hardest cases: dialect variation, code-switching, and long-form audio. Tops the Open Universal Arabic ASR leaderboard at 25.87 WER / 11.80 CER average, beating 7B-class rivals, with native transformers support and vLLM integration for production serving.
why: Cohere's lab releases usually ship under non-commercial licenses; this one is explicitly Apache-2.0. A frontier lab giving one underserved language its full attention, under a license that lets anyone build on the result.
useCases: Transcription across Arabic dialects, not just MSA
Voice interfaces and captioning for Arabic-speaking users
Long-form audio transcription pipelines via vLLM
audience: Speech engineers, Arabic-language product teams, accessibility builders

setup.requirements: Hugging Face transformers on a modest GPU; vLLM recipes provided for production serving

license.notes: A notable departure — CohereLabs models have typically been CC-BY-NC. The model card states plainly that this release is governed by Apache 2.0.

limitations: Arabic-only by design, and brand-new — little community mileage yet, so evaluate on your specific dialect before trusting it in production.
take: Open ASR has been English-first since forever. This is what it looks like when that stops being taken for granted.
